hypothesis: If equivariance-inducing regularization (equigrad) causes a non-equivariant architecture to converge toward the same representational manifold as architecturally equivariant models, then systematically interpolating the strength of this regularization during training will reveal a monotonic relationship between the degree of learned quasi-equivariance and cross-model representational alignment, such that a critical regularization threshold exists below which alignment drops sharply, providing a quantitative criterion for the minimum inductive bias required to achieve foundation-level representational universality without full architectural equivariance.
